meta.title: Writing Your First Tests with Vitest
meta.excerpt: Learn test-driven development basics by writing unit tests for a React component

props.text: Add Vitest and React Testing Library to your project.

props.code:
# Install testing dependencies
npm install -D vitest @vitejs/plugin-react jsdom
npm install -D @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom @testing-library/user-event

# Create vitest.config.ts in your project root

props.title: 💡 Hint
props.body: Vitest is a fast, Vite-powered test runner that works great with modern React projects.

props.text: Create the configuration file for Vitest.
Set up testing utilities that run before each test.

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: This import adds useful matchers like toBeInTheDocument() and toHaveTextContent().

props.text: Create a test file for a simple Button component.

props.title: 📖 Explanation
props.body: We use vi.fn() to create a mock function, then verify it was called after clicking the button.

props.text: Add a test script and run your tests.

props.code:
# Add to package.json scripts:
"scripts": {
  "test": "vitest",
  "test:run": "vitest run"
}

# Run tests in watch mode:
npm test

# Run tests once:
npm run test:run

# Expected output:
# ✓ components/button.test.tsx (2)
#   ✓ Button (2)
#     ✓ renders with correct text
#     ✓ calls onClick when clicked

props.title: 💡 Hint
props.body: Watch mode reruns tests automatically when you change files - great for TDD!
